agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Leave the sauna and enter the pool room. Stand on the platform at the end of the pool near the lounge chairs. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I see the doorway to the pool room on the right side (+90°). Let me turn right and head through that door.

message.content: I see the doorway to the pool room on the right side (+90°). Let me turn right and head through that door.

messages.content: I'm now looking into the pool room. I need to move forward through the doorway into the pool area.

message.content: I'm now looking into the pool room. I need to move forward through the doorway into the pool area.

messages.content: I can see lounge chairs in the left view. Let me turn to face that direction and move there.

message.content: I can see lounge chairs in the left view. Let me turn to face that direction and move there.

messages.content: This looks good - I can see the lounge chairs area. Let me move forward along this corridor toward them.

message.content: This looks good - I can see the lounge chairs area. Let me move forward along this corridor toward them.

messages.content: I'm now facing the pool. I can see lounge chairs to the left. Let me continue toward the end of the pool where the lounge chairs are.

message.content: I'm now facing the pool. I can see lounge chairs to the left. Let me continue toward the end of the pool where the lounge chairs are.

messages.content: I can see the lounge chairs area clearly in the left view. Let me turn and head there.

message.content: I can see the lounge chairs area clearly in the left view. Let me turn and head there.

messages.content: Now I can see the lounge chairs clearly on the right side, near what appears to be the platform at the end of the pool. Let me move closer to them.

message.content: Now I can see the lounge chairs clearly on the right side, near what appears to be the platform at the end of the pool. Let me move closer to them.

messages.content: This position looks good - I'm at the platform at the end of the pool, with the lounge chairs nearby behind me. Let me stop here.

message.content: This position looks good - I'm at the platform at the end of the pool, with the lounge chairs nearby behind me. Let me stop here.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 73 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 27, "tool_calls_remaining": 73}

info.message: STOP not executed (first request). You still have 73 tool calls — verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.

messages.content: This looks like a good position - I'm at the platform at the end of the pool, with lounge chairs right behind me. This matches the goal description well.

message.content: This looks like a good position - I'm at the platform at the end of the pool, with lounge chairs right behind me. This matches the goal description well.
